sections.paragraph:
μιν
Etym. ionic acc. sg. of the pron. of the 3rd pers. (v. ἵ) through all genders, for αὐτόν, αὐτήν, αὐτο
Etym. always enclitic, Hom., Hdt.; doric and attic νιν

I. Hom. joins μὶν αὐτόν himself, as a stronger form; but αὐτόν μιν is reflexive, oneself, for ἑαυτόν, Od.

II. rarely as 3 pers. pl. for αὐτούς, αὐτάς, αὐτά.
